proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Midostaurin vs no-midostaurin ORR 0.169 (n=7545) vs 0.169 (n=42455); difference -0.001, chi-square p=0.92.
Gilteritinib vs no-gilteritinib ORR 0.163 vs 0.170; diff -0.007, chi-square p=0.13.
Ivosidenib vs no-ivosidenib ORR 0.171 vs 0.169; diff +0.002, chi-square p=0.76.
Enasidenib vs no-enasidenib ORR 0.161 vs 0.170; diff -0.009, chi-square p=0.17.
Venetoclax/azacitidine vs not ORR 0.173 (n=20020) vs 0.166 (n=29980); diff +0.007, chi-square p=0.031.
7+3 vs no-7+3 ORR 0.170 vs 0.168; diff +0.002, chi-square p=0.54.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation have a higher rate of objective_response than patients without npm1_mutation (favorable-risk AML marker).
Patients with tp53_mutation have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without tp53_mutation (adverse-risk marker).
Patients with complex_karyotype have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without complex_karyotype.
Patients with idh1_mutation have a higher rate of objective_response than patients without idh1_mutation.
Patients with idh2_mutation have a higher rate of objective_response than patients without idh2_mutation.
Patients with flt3_itd have a different rate of objective_response than patients without flt3_itd.
Patients with secondary_aml have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with de novo AML.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ vs NPM1- ORR 0.173 vs 0.167; diff +0.006, chi-square p=0.11.
TP53+ vs TP53- ORR 0.166 vs 0.169; diff -0.004, chi-square p=0.52. No detectable adverse main effect.
Complex-karyotype+ vs - ORR 0.168 vs 0.169; diff -0.001, chi-square p=0.82. No main effect.
IDH1+ vs IDH1- ORR 0.221 vs 0.165; diff +0.056, chi-square p=3.5e-18. Strong favorable main effect.
IDH2+ vs IDH2- ORR 0.165 vs 0.169; diff -0.004, chi-square p=0.50.
FLT3-ITD+ vs FLT3-ITD- ORR 0.172 vs 0.168; diff +0.004, chi-square p=0.40.
Secondary AML vs de novo ORR 0.169 vs 0.169; diff 0.000, chi-square p=0.91.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response (negative log-odds slope).
Older age_years is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different rate of objective_response than male patients.
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a lower rate of objective_response than fit patients.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression of objective_response on ecog_ps: log-odds slope -0.384 per ECOG unit (OR 0.68), Wald p < 1e-97. ORR drops from 0.213 (ECOG 0) to 0.155 (ECOG 1) to 0.112 (ECOG 2).
Logistic regression of objective_response on age_years: slope +0.0004 per year, p=0.72. No detectable main age effect.
Female vs male ORR 0.171 vs 0.168; diff +0.003, chi-square p=0.33.
Unfit vs fit ORR 0.169 vs 0.169; diff +0.001, chi-square p=0.84. The 'unfit_for_intensive' label is not directly prognostic by itself.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin yields a higher rate of objective_response than no midostaurin (positive treatment-by-mutation interaction).
Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib yields a higher rate of objective_response than no gilteritinib.
The benefit of treatment_midostaurin on objective_response is greater in FLT3-mutated (flt3_itd or flt3_tkd) patients than in FLT3-wild-type patients.
The benefit of treatment_gilteritinib on objective_response is greater in FLT3-mutated (flt3_itd or flt3_tkd) patients than in FLT3-wild-type patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within FLT3-ITD+ (n=10000): midostaurin ORR 0.161 (n=1468) vs 0.174 (n=8532), numerically worse. Logistic interaction coef -0.105, p=0.22. No FLT3-ITD-specific midostaurin benefit detected.
Within FLT3-ITD+: gilteritinib ORR 0.169 vs 0.172, diff -0.003. Interaction coef +0.034, p=0.69. No FLT3-ITD-specific gilteritinib benefit detected.
Within FLT3-any+: midostaurin ORR 0.162 vs 0.171; within FLT3-wt: 0.171 vs 0.168. Interaction coef -0.082, p=0.29. No differential midostaurin benefit by FLT3 status.
Within FLT3-any+: gilteritinib ORR 0.163 vs 0.171; within FLT3-wt: 0.163 vs 0.170. Interaction coef -0.010, p=0.89. No differential gilteritinib benefit by FLT3 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib yields a higher rate of objective_response than no ivosidenib (positive interaction expected from drug mechanism).
Among patients with idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib yields a higher rate of objective_response than no enasidenib.
treatment_ivosidenib does not yield a higher response among idh2_mutation patients (off-target negative control).
treatment_enasidenib does not yield a higher response among idh1_mutation patients (off-target negative control).

analyses.result_summary: Within IDH1+ (n=3624): ivosidenib ORR 0.174 (n=298) vs 0.225 (n=3326); within IDH1-: 0.171 vs 0.164. Interaction coef -0.364, p=0.027. Ivosidenib is associated with NUMERICALLY LOWER response among IDH1-mutated patients (opposite of expected direction).

Within IDH2+: enasidenib ORR 0.144 (n=354) vs 0.167 (n=4325); within IDH2-: 0.163 vs 0.170. Interaction coef -0.124, p=0.45. No enasidenib-by-IDH2 benefit detected; if anything numerically worse on enasidenib.
Within IDH2+: ivosidenib ORR 0.175 vs 0.165, diff +0.010. Interaction coef +0.062, p=0.69. As expected, no off-target ivosidenib benefit in IDH2.
Within IDH1+: enasidenib ORR 0.233 vs 0.220, diff +0.013. Interaction coef +0.149, p=0.33. No off-target enasidenib effect in IDH1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients flagged unfit_for_intensive, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine yields a higher rate of objective_response than no venetoclax/azacitidine (positive interaction reflecting drug's intended population).
Among patients flagged unfit_for_intensive, treatment_7plus3 does not improve (and may worsen) objective_response relative to no 7+3.
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine yields a larger absolute increase in objective_response among patients aged >=65 than among patients aged <65.

analyses.result_summary: Within unfit (n=20056): VEN/AZA ORR 0.179 (n=8013) vs 0.163 (n=12043), within-stratum chi-square p=0.0029. Within fit: 0.170 vs 0.168, NS. Interaction coef +0.104, p=0.036. Confirms VEN/AZA preferentially benefits unfit subgroup.
Within unfit: 7+3 ORR 0.169 vs 0.170, diff -0.001; within fit: 0.171 vs 0.167. Interaction coef -0.033, p=0.50. No 7+3-specific harm or benefit by fitness.
Within age>=65: VEN/AZA ORR 0.174 vs 0.166; within age<65: 0.172 vs 0.166. Interaction coef +0.010, p=0.83. The VEN/AZA benefit is not specifically driven by age >=65.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for ECOG, age, sex, AML mutations, cytogenetics, blood counts and treatment assignments, idh1_mutation remains independently positively associated with objective_response (signed log-odds > 0).
After multivariable adjustment, ecog_ps remains independently negatively associated with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains independently positively associated with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, higher albumin_g_dl is independently positively associated with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, higher blast_pct_marrow is independently negatively associated with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, higher wbc_k_per_ul is independently negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ age_years + sex_female + ecog_ps + secondary_aml + unfit_for_intensive + complex_karyotype + flt3_itd + flt3_tkd + idh1_mutation + idh2_mutation + npm1_mutation + tp53_mutation + wbc_k_per_ul + blast_pct_marrow + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + treatment_midostaurin + treatment_gilteritinib + treatment_ivosidenib + treatment_enasidenib + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine + treatment_7plus3', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression (n=50000). Significant independent predictors: ecog_ps coef=-0.384 (p<1e-90); idh1_mutation coef=+0.360 (p<1e-15); albumin_g_dl coef=+0.118 (p=1.4e-6); blast_pct_marrow coef=-0.0057 (p=1.2e-19); wbc_k_per_ul coef=-0.0022 (p=1.9e-5); treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine coef=+0.058 (p=0.018). Other treatments and other mutations (npm1, tp53, complex karyotype, flt3) all NS.
Adjusted idh1_mutation log-odds = +0.360, OR=1.43, p=2.2e-17. IDH1 effect persists after full adjustment.
Adjusted ecog_ps log-odds = -0.384, OR=0.68, Wald p<1e-90. ECOG effect persists after full adjustment.
Adjusted treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine log-odds = +0.058, OR=1.06, p=0.018. Modest but significant adjusted effect.
Adjusted albumin_g_dl log-odds = +0.118 per g/dL, p=1.4e-6.
Adjusted blast_pct_marrow log-odds = -0.0057 per percent, p=1.2e-19.
Adjusted wbc_k_per_ul log-odds = -0.0022 per k/uL, p=1.9e-5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tp53_mutation reduces the benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response (negative interaction).
tp53_mutation reduces the benefit of treatment_7plus3 on objective_response (negative interaction).
complex_karyotype reduces the benefit of treatment_7plus3 on objective_response (negative interaction; complex-karyotype AML is chemo-refractory).
complex_karyotype changes the benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Within TP53+: VEN/AZA ORR 0.164 vs 0.167, diff -0.003; within TP53-: 0.175 vs 0.166. Interaction coef -0.087, p=0.28. Direction consistent with TP53 abolishing VEN/AZA benefit but NS.
Within TP53+: 7+3 ORR 0.164 vs 0.167; within TP53-: 0.171 vs 0.168. Interaction coef -0.044, p=0.59. NS.

Within complex-karyotype+: 7+3 ORR 0.155 (n=3415) vs 0.179 (n=4047), within-stratum chi-square p=0.0084. Within complex-: 0.172 vs 0.167. Interaction coef -0.213, p=0.0016. 7+3 is associated with WORSE response in complex-karyotype patients (significant negative interaction).
Interaction coef +0.035, p=0.61. No detectable interaction between VEN/AZA and complex karyotype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable albumin_g_dl: log-odds slope +0.115 per g/dL, OR=1.12, p=1.4e-6.
Univariable blast_pct_marrow: slope -0.00567 per percent, p=1.2e-19.
Univariable wbc_k_per_ul: slope -0.00216 per k/uL, p=1.9e-5.
Univariable crp_mg_l: slope -0.0070 per mg/L, p=2.8e-6.
Univariable weight_loss_pct_6mo: slope -0.0401 per %, p=7.4e-35. Strong association.
Univariable ldh_u_l: slope -0.00014 per U/L, p=0.35. NS.
Univariable appetite_loss_grade: slope -0.0233 per grade, p=0.038.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 24 candidate SNPs (snp_*) shows a significant association with objective_response after Bonferroni correction (alpha = 0.05/24 = 0.0021).
Race/ethnicity is associated with objective_response (omnibus test); white patients have a higher unadjusted ORR than non-white groups.
Insurance type is associated with objective_response (omnibus chi-square).
Patients with rural_residence have a different objective_response rate than urban patients.
Higher education_years is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code: for snp in [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith('snp_')]: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[snp], df.objective_response))
analyses.result_summary: Screened 24 SNPs. Smallest p-value snp_rs1050828 (ORR 0.185 vs 0.168, p=0.0056). No SNP passes Bonferroni alpha=0.0021. No reproducible single-SNP signal.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(race_ethnicity)', df).fit() and adjusted version
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ORR by race: white 0.173, hispanic 0.167, asian 0.163, black 0.157, other 0.147 (LR omnibus p=0.0087). After adjusting for ecog_ps and age_years, no race category differs significantly from the asian reference (all p>=0.15). The unadjusted gap is largely explained by performance status / age confounding.
ORR by insurance: medicare 0.172, medicaid 0.169, private 0.167, uninsured 0.166. Differences small and not significant (chi-square p>0.1).
Rural vs urban ORR essentially identical (both 0.169), p=1.00.
Univariable education_years log-odds slope +0.00039 per year, p=0.92. NS.
Univariable smoking_pack_years log-odds slope +0.00032 per pack-year, p=0.51. NS.
